name: LLM Provider APIs
description: APIs for sending inference requests to large language models — first-party model APIs, cloud model-hosting platforms, routing gateways, and enterprise assistant platforms, compared across access, model breadth, request features, multimodal support, advanced capabilities, compatibility, governance, and pricing

groups.name: General Information
groups.description: Provider, product shape, lifecycle status, and basic identifying information

attributes.name: Provider
attributes.description: Company or org operating the API (Anthropic, Amazon Web Services, Google, dust.tt, etc.)

attributes.name: Category
attributes.description: The product shape; single primary tag expected but multi-select allowed for hybrids

attributes.name: API Launched
attributes.description: First public/GA availability of the inference API (not the company founding)

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Overall availability of the developer API

attributes.name: API Docs
attributes.description: Official API reference / developer documentation URL

attributes.name: Website
attributes.description: Official product/homepage URL

groups.name: Access & Authentication
groups.description: How you obtain access, authenticate, and where requests are served

attributes.name: Auth Methods
attributes.description: How requests are authenticated; multi-select

attributes.name: Self-Serve Signup
attributes.description: Can a developer get a working API key without sales contact or an existing cloud account?

attributes.name: Free Credits / Trial
attributes.description: Are there free credits or a usable free tier for evaluation (not merely a demo playground)?

attributes.name: Regional Endpoints
attributes.description: Where the API can be called / served from

attributes.name: Data Residency Options
attributes.description: Can the customer pin data processing to a chosen region/jurisdiction? A contractual/config guarantee, not merely multiple endpoints

groups.name: Model Access
groups.description: Model breadth, open weights, version pinning, and custom deployment

attributes.name: Model Breadth
attributes.description: How many model families are reachable through this one API

attributes.name: Open-Weight Models
attributes.description: Are open-weight models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, DeepSeek, etc.) available through the API?

attributes.name: Model Version Pinning
attributes.description: Can the caller pin a specific dated/immutable model snapshot vs only a floating alias that may change under them?

attributes.name: Custom / Fine-Tuned Deployment
attributes.description: Can the customer deploy and call their own fine-tuned or private model through the same API?

attributes.name: Model Catalog Size
attributes.description: Rough count of distinct models callable; note the exact figure and check date in the comment

attributes.name: Chat / Messages Endpoint
attributes.description: Offers a modern multi-turn chat/messages inference endpoint (not legacy single-prompt completion only)

attributes.name: Streaming
attributes.description: Supports token streaming (SSE or equivalent) for incremental responses

attributes.name: Tool / Function Calling
attributes.description: Model can request structured tool/function invocations the caller executes (shipped, documented support only)

attributes.name: Parallel Tool Calls
attributes.description: Model can emit multiple tool calls in a single turn for concurrent execution

attributes.name: Structured Output
attributes.description: Strongest guaranteed-shape output — server-enforced JSON schema, valid-JSON mode, constrained decoding/grammar, or prompt-only

attributes.name: System Prompt
attributes.description: Distinct system/developer role or instruction field supported at the API level

attributes.name: Image Input (Vision)
attributes.description: Accepts images as request input for the LLM to reason over

attributes.name: Audio Input
attributes.description: Accepts audio input (speech understanding / transcription via the same provider API)

attributes.name: Document / PDF Input
attributes.description: Native document/PDF ingestion as a first-class input type (not just pasted text)

attributes.name: Image Generation
attributes.description: Offers an image-generation endpoint through the same API/account

attributes.name: Text-to-Speech / Audio Output
attributes.description: Offers speech/audio synthesis output through the same API/account

attributes.name: Embeddings Endpoint
attributes.description: Offers a text-embeddings endpoint for retrieval/semantic search under the same API

groups.name: Advanced Capabilities
groups.description: Caching, batch, long context, reasoning, built-in tools, MCP, retrieval, and agents

attributes.name: Prompt Caching
attributes.description: Supports caching of repeated prompt prefixes for cost/latency reduction (explicit or automatic; note which)

attributes.name: Batch API
attributes.description: Offers an asynchronous batch/offline endpoint (typically discounted, higher-latency) for bulk jobs

attributes.name: Max Context Window
attributes.description: Largest input context (tokens) any model on the API supports; higher is better. Record the enabling model in the comment

attributes.name: Extended Reasoning
attributes.description: Exposes a reasoning/thinking mode or reasoning-effort control at the API level

attributes.name: Built-in Web Search
attributes.description: Provider offers a first-party web-search / grounding tool callable via the API

attributes.name: Built-in Code Execution
attributes.description: Provider offers a first-party code-interpreter / sandboxed execution tool via the API

attributes.name: MCP Support
attributes.description: API can connect to Model Context Protocol servers (hosted/remote MCP connectors) as part of a request or agent

attributes.name: Files / Retrieval API
attributes.description: Offers file upload + managed retrieval/RAG or a files API the model can reference

attributes.name: Agents / Assistants API
attributes.description: Offers a higher-level stateful agents/assistants/threads API above raw inference

groups.name: Compatibility & SDKs
groups.description: Interop standards and official client libraries

attributes.name: OpenAI-Compatible API
attributes.description: Exposes an OpenAI-Chat-Completions-compatible endpoint so existing OpenAI SDK code works with a base-URL swap

attributes.name: Official SDKs
attributes.description: First-party maintained client libraries; multi-select

attributes.name: Native API Style
attributes.description: The provider's own primary request/response shape (before any compatibility shim)

attributes.name: Webhooks / Callbacks
attributes.description: Supports webhooks or async callbacks for long-running / batch job completion

groups.name: Reliability & Operations
groups.description: SLA, rate limits, fallback routing, and status transparency

attributes.name: Published SLA
attributes.description: A contractual uptime SLA is offered (often paid/enterprise tier); note the figure/tier in the comment

attributes.name: Rate Limit Model
attributes.description: How throughput limits work — fixed per-tier caps, auto-scaling with spend, reserved capacity, or negotiated

attributes.name: Provider Fallback / Routing
attributes.description: Can automatically route or fall back across underlying providers/models on error or capacity (a defining gateway feature)

attributes.name: Status Page
attributes.description: Public status/uptime page exists

groups.name: Governance & Compliance
groups.description: Data handling defaults, certifications, and enterprise access control

attributes.name: No-Training Default
attributes.description: API inputs/outputs are NOT used to train models by default (zero-training). Consumer defaults that may train are false

attributes.name: Zero Data Retention
attributes.description: A zero-retention mode (no storage of prompts/outputs beyond the request) is available, at least on request/enterprise

attributes.name: Compliance Certifications
attributes.description: Documented certifications/attestations; multi-select

attributes.name: Enterprise SSO / Access Control
attributes.description: Offers SSO/SAML and org-level access controls for API/console governance

groups.name: Pricing & Billing
groups.description: How you pay, unified billing, and discounts

attributes.name: Pricing Model
attributes.description: How you pay — per-token usage, flat subscription, reserved throughput, gateway markup, or a free tier; multi-select

attributes.name: Unified Billing
attributes.description: One bill/account covers all reachable models/providers (a gateway/platform advantage)

attributes.name: Cache/Batch Discount
attributes.description: Offers a price discount for cached prompts and/or batch jobs; note the rate in the comment

attributes.name: Cheapest Entry
attributes.description: The lowest-friction way to start paying; enterprise-only when no self-serve billing exists
